title: Response
description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema0.title: Reference Object
__schema0.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema1.title: Reference
__schema1.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema5.title: summary
__schema5.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema6.title: description
__schema6.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema8.title: Response
__schema8.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema12.title: Header
__schema12.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema13.title: Header
__schema13.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema14.title: description
__schema14.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema15.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema15.title: required

__schema17.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema17.title: deprecated

__schema19.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema19.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema21.title: style
__schema21.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema27.title: Schema Object
__schema27.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema28.title: Type Array Schema
__schema28.description: OpenAPI 3.1 (JSON Schema) allows type to be an array of primitive types.

__schema29.title: title
__schema29.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema30.title: description
__schema30.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema31.title: default
__schema31.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema32.title: example
__schema32.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema35.title: enum
__schema35.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema37.title: const
__schema37.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema39.title: Discriminator
__schema39.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema40.title: propertyName
__schema40.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema41.title: mapping
__schema41.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema46.title: XML
__schema46.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema47.title: name
__schema47.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema48.title: namespace
__schema48.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema49.title: prefix
__schema49.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema50.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema50.title: attribute

__schema52.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema52.title: wrapped

__schema56.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema56.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema57.title: description
__schema57.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema58.title: url
__schema58.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema60.title: readOnly
__schema60.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema61.title: writeOnly
__schema61.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema62.title: deprecated
__schema62.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema66.title: String Object
__schema66.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema68.title: format
__schema68.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema69.title: Pattern
__schema69.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema76.title: contentMediaType
__schema76.description: Media type of the encoded string content.

__schema78.title: Number Object
__schema78.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema80.title: format
__schema80.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema81.title: multipleOf
__schema81.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema83.title: minimum
__schema83.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema85.title: maximum
__schema85.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema87.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema87.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema89.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema89.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema91.title: Integer Object
__schema91.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema93.title: format
__schema93.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema94.title: multipleOf
__schema94.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema96.title: minimum
__schema96.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema98.title: maximum
__schema98.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema100.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema100.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema102.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema102.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema104.title: Boolean Object
__schema104.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema106.title: Null Object
__schema106.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema108.title: Array Object
__schema108.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema112.title: prefixItems
__schema112.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema114.title: contains
__schema114.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema126.title: Object Object
__schema126.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema150.title: Composition Schema
__schema150.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema151.title: allOf
__schema151.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema153.title: anyOf
__schema153.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema155.title: oneOf
__schema155.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema157.title: not
__schema157.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema159.title: if
__schema159.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema163.title: else
__schema163.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema174.title: externalValue
__schema174.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema182.title: MediaType
__schema182.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema190.title: Encoding
__schema190.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema191.title: contentType
__schema191.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema207.title: Link
__schema207.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema208.title: Link
__schema208.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema209.title: operationId
__schema209.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema210.title: operationRef
__schema210.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema211.title: parameters
__schema211.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema214.title: requestBody
__schema214.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema217.title: url
__schema217.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema218.title: description
__schema218.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema219.title: variables
__schema219.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema221.title: ServerVariable
__schema221.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema222.title: enum
__schema222.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema224.title: default
__schema224.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema225.title: description
__schema225.description: An optional description for the server variable.
